user_symptom: we experienced increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on metrics extracted using log filters.

we experienced increased error rates with the CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics and GetMetricData APIs when requesting metrics older than 3 hours in the US-EAST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may have transitioned into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if the period of the alarm was longer than 3 hours.
customers experienced intermittent delays in the availability of CloudWatch metrics for load balancers in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.

some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch Metrics PutMetricData and CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents APIs in the AP-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state, and some metric data may be missing.

Elevated query error rates and delays for CloudWatch Logs Insights in US-EAST-1 Region. Customers running queries using the StartQuery API may not be able to successfully schedule the query execution. Some customers experienced increased delays for log events in query results in CloudWatch Logs Insights.
Users experienced increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs, as well as some delayed log events.

We are investigating increased faults and latencies for CloudWatch APIs and metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics. ...some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch APIs in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

a small number of instances were unavailable due to power loss in a single Availability Zone (usw1-az1) in the US-WEST-1 Region. During this time, some customers also experienced insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks for instances that were not impacted by the power event. During this time, CloudWatch alarms may have transitioned into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on the delayed metrics.

we experienced degraded performance for some EBS volumes in the US-EAST-2 Region. During this time, some customers also experienced insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks. CloudWatch alarms may have transitioned into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on the delayed metrics.
customers may have experienced elevated alarms API faults and delayed alarms in the EU-WEST-2 Region.

Customers experienced increased faults for CloudWatch Logs APIs and increased delays for CloudWatch log events. Metrics filtered from those log events were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics may have transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

We are investigating elevated rate of API faults and delays in processing some alarms in the US-EAST-1 Region. We can confirm that scheduled events are not being delivered via CloudWatch Events in the US-EAST-1 Region. AWS Lambda functions that are invoked in response to CloudWatch events may not be getting invoked successfully.

Some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.
customers experienced elevated API fault rates and latencies in the EU-WEST-2 Region. Some metrics were also delayed initially.

some customers may have experienced intermittent delays in the availability of CloudWatch ELB metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics.

we experienced increased API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-1 Region. APIs also returned insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks and CloudWatch alarms transitioned into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on the delayed metrics.
we experienced increased connection errors and latencies for CloudWatch APIs. During this time CloudWatch alarms may have transitioned into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics.

We are investigating increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions in the US-EAST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on metrics extracted using log filters.
We are investigating increased error rates and delays for CloudWatch Logs Insights queries in the US-EAST-2 Region.
customers experienced elevated errors listing metrics via the CloudWatch ListMetrics API and the CloudWatch Management Console in the US-WEST-2 Region.

Customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Log events and metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state. Customers will experience faults in the CloudWatch Logs Management Console.

some customers experienced increased delays for CloudWatch metrics extracted from CloudWatch Logs in the AP-SOUTHEAST-2 Region. CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics may have transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

We are investigating increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region. Log events and metrics may be delayed. Alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics.
customers experienced increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms in the US-EAST-1 Region.

Scheduled events are not being delivered via CloudWatch Events in the US-WEST-2 Region. AWS Lambda functions that are invoked in response to CloudWatch events may not be getting invoked successfully. Starting at 5:27 PM PDT, there were delays delivering scheduled CloudWatch events.
We are investigating increased faults and latencies for CloudWatch APIs and metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics.

some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into the INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

We are investigating increased provisioning latencies for new load balancers, delayed propagation of load balancer CloudWatch metrics, and inconsistent health check statuses from the DescribeTargetHealth API for Network Load Balancers in the US-EAST-1 Region.

customers experienced elevated faults when making GetMetricStatistics API requests for metrics data more than two days old in the US-EAST-1 Region and CloudWatch Dashboards were unavailable. CloudWatch logs customers experienced elevated API faults and delayed log events.
we experienced latency in the ability to search contacts, and the inability to add contacts to rooms and calls in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Between 3:19 PM and 5:58 PM PST we experienced increased API error rates in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates and latency for the Identity, Registry, and Rules Engine APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased invoke and API error rates in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates and latencies for invokes in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced elevated latency and API error rates for the Amazon EC2 Container Service APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region.

Between 7:31 AM PST on December 7 and 2:20 AM PST on December 8, ECS experienced increased API error rates, latencies, and task launch failures. ECS customers using the Fargate launch type along with EKS customers using Fargate continued to see decreasing impact in the form of insufficient capacity errors.

We are currently experiencing increased error rates in the GetRecommendations API in the US-EAST-1 Region. Between 12:36 PM and 1:42 PM PST, we experienced increased error rates in the GetRecommendations API in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased API error rates in AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
we experienced increased error rates for the AWS Management Console in the US-WEST-2 Region.
a subset of Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics applications were not processing data in US-EAST-1 Region.
Between March 2 10:03 PM and March 3 12:30 AM PST, the VPC Management Console experienced elevated error rates in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates for ELB APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.
Between 12:43 PM and 12:59 PM PST, we experienced increased error rates and latencies for some AWS services within the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs, and elevated launch failures for new EC2 instances in the US-WEST-2 Region.
we experienced elevated failure rate for sample app and custom SDK generation and cloud logic API creation for all Mobile Hub projects.
we experienced elevated API error rate in the US-EAST-2 Region, restricted to control plane operations for API Gateway Version 2 API (WebSocket and HTTP APIs).
we experienced increased error rates affecting the Billing Console including the Dashboard and the Bills page.
we experienced increased API error rates and latencies as well as increased back-end instance registration times in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Between 9:37 AM PST and 5:20 PM PST we experienced an increased error rate while executing deployments, in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced elevated API error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased API error rates for the ECR APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Between 12:40 AM and 3:42 AM PST, we experienced delays in running steps, as well as starting and terminating Amazon EMR clusters in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Beginning at 11:43 AM PST some Amazon Aurora clusters experienced increased database create times and cluster unavailability in the CA-CENTRAL-1 Region.

We are investigating CloudWatch Metric Streams delivery delays in the AF-SOUTH-1 Region. We can confirm elevated API error rates for CloudWatch Metric Streams APIs in the AF-SOUTH-1 Region. Customers may experience issues creating or updating their metric streams.
Between 3:51 PM and 4:57 PM PST we experienced increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region.
Between 3:19 PM and 4:43 PM PST we experienced increased API fault rates and latencies in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.

customers experienced a significant impairment to their Amazon Kinesis Data Streams API operations. As a result customers are not able to write or read data published to Kinesis streams. Customers may continue to experience increased latencies and failure rates.
some Amazon Aurora clusters experienced increased database create times and cluster unavailability in the AP-SOUTHEAST-2 Region.
we experienced increased error rates for binding certificates and first time issuance in the US-EAST-1 Region. We also saw delays in validation e-mails in all regions.
we experienced increased error rates affecting a small number of customers running Amazon Athena queries in the US-EAST-1 Region.

Customers experienced elevated latency and error rates in response to IAM API requests, as well as delays in describing recently created or modified IAM resources. In addition, propagation of IAM API updates was delayed, and newly created or recently updated IAM users, credentials, roles, and policies may not have been available for authentication and authorization in those regions.
S3 customers saw elevated errors and latency for a subset of API requests to create and manage Amazon S3 buckets.

We are investigating increased error rates in the AWS SSO management console in the US-EAST-1 Region. Affected customers experiencing issues when attempting SSO authentication for the AWS console and services.
We are investigating increased errors when calling WorkSpaces APIs and connecting to WorkSpaces in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates for API calls in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Between 3:03 AM and 5:42 AM PDT, customers may have experienced some delayed alarms in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased Workmail Outlook latencies in the US-EAST-1 Region impacting the sending and receiving of new messages or appointments in Outlook clients. Between 10:16 AM and 11:07 AM PDT we experienced degraded WorkMail Outlook performances in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates and latencies affecting multiple services and the AWS Console in the SA-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced elevated API error rates and connection failures in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased API error rates in the US-EAST-1 Region.
increased errors when launching new WorkSpaces in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Between 2:04 AM and 7:50 AM PDT we experienced increased API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-2 Region.
We are investigating increased Git Push/Pull error rates via the SSH protocol in the US-EAST-2 Region.
Between 2:01 AM PDT and 5:07 AM PDT we experienced increased error rates for send and receive operations for FIFO Queues in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates for Amazon S3 requests in the US-EAST-2 Region.
Between 6:53 PM and 9:37 PM PDT we experienced elevated API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates for AWS Lambda calls from Amazon Lex bots in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Beginning at 5:20 AM PDT, we experienced increased queue latency for Build operations in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Customers may have experienced increased API error rates, 500 Errors, or "Request Limit Exceeded" when calling an affected API or using the EC2 Management Console.

We are investigating increased API error rates and latencies in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region. Between 3:46 PM and 5:08 PM PDT we experienced slightly increased latencies for the EC2 APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
We are investigating authentication issues affecting workspace availability in the US-WEST-2 Region
customers may have experienced elevated latency and errors when accessing AWS APIs and consoles in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
Failures in starting clusters and increased error rates impacting start up of clusters operations.
We are investigating increased API error rates for the SubscribeToShard API in the US-WEST-2 Region.
Between 1:10 PM PDT and 5:26 PM PDT we experienced increased error rates in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.
We are experiencing increased latencies while provisioning new ElastiCache nodes and elevated API error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased API error rates when establishing new connections to AWS IoT Core in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Between 1:52 AM and 3:35 AM PDT we experienced increased API error rates and latencies in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased invoke timeout rates for AWS Lambda functions in the EU-WEST-1 Region. We are still investigating increased invoke timeout rates and elevated latencies for a small proportion of AWS Lambda functions in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Between 10:23 AM and 1:11 PM PDT we experienced increased error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

We are investigating delayed ENI attachment times for EC2 instances within a single Availability Zone (USE1-AZ4) in the US-EAST-1 Region. Newly launched EC2 instances or new ENI attachments, may experience delay in establishing network connectivity within the affected Availability Zone.
we experienced power and network connectivity issues for some instances, and degraded performance for some EBS volumes in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
We are investigating network connectivity issues for some instances and increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs within the US-EAST-2 Region.
customers experienced elevated DNS failures when querying Route 53 Resolver for certain hostnames in a single Availability Zone (use1-az4) in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Between 10:22 PM and 10:39 PM PDT we experienced degraded performance for a small number of EBS volumes in a Single Availability zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced connectivity issues affecting some database instances in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-2 Region.
we experienced elevated packet loss to and from some destinations on the Internet in the SA-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating network connectivity issues for some instances in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region. We can confirm that some instances are unreachable and some EBS volumes are experiencing degraded performance in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased error rates for (some) new launches in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region. Between July 21 11:45 PM and July 22 12:56 AM PDT we experienced increased error rates for instance launches in the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating connectivity issues for some EC2 instances in a single Availability Zone (euc1-az1) in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region. We can confirm increased API error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs and connectivity issues for instances within a single Availability Zone (euc1-az1) within the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region. Some EBS volumes are also experiencing degraded performance within the affected Availability Zone.

Between 5:33 PM and 7:06 PM PST some instances experienced connectivity issues within a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-2 Region. The affected instances did not lose power but became unreachable due to a loss of power to networking devices within the affected Availability Zone. Some EBS volumes experienced degraded performance during this time period and launches of new EC2 instances and the creation of new EBS volumes experienced increased error rates.

Storage Gateway VMs appear to be offline and are not able to perform out of cache reads or upload to our service. The gateways will continue to accept writes but will not be able to proceed to upload them to our service until this issue is resolved.
we experienced increased error rates for instance launches in a single availability zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Between 12:30 AM and 7:10 AM PST we experience periods of network connectivity issues for NAT Gateway within the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.

We are investigating internet connectivity issues in the US-GOV-EAST-1 Region. We can confirm Internet and EC2 Public IP address connectivity issues for newly launched EC2 instances within the US-GOV-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating connectivity issues affecting some instances in a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. We can confirm that some instances are impaired and some EBS volumes are experiencing degraded performance within a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. Some EC2 APIs are also experiencing increased error rates and latencies.
customers experienced elevated network packet loss that impacted connectivity to a subset of Internet destinations.
we experienced network connectivity issues affecting Direct Connect customers using the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
some database instances in the AP-SOUTH-1 Region are experiencing degraded performance or are unavailable.
a small number of instances experienced connectivity issues in a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
Between 8:26 AM and 9:46 AM PST, some customers experienced connectivity issues from the East Coast of the United States to AWS services.
We are investigating increased API and launch error rates in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased API error rates for the EC2 API, and connectivity issues between EC2 instances and AWS services in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Between 10:24 AM PDT and 11:31 AM PDT, we experienced meeting availability issues in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Between August 22 8:36 PM and August 23 6:05 AM PDT, some RDS instances experienced connectivity issues within a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.
This may be impacting Internet connectivity between some customer networks and the AP-SOUTH-1 Region.

Storage Gateway VMs appear to be offline and are not able to perform out of cache reads or upload to our service. The gateways will continue to accept writes but will not be able to proceed to upload them to our service until this issue is resolved.

We are investigating increased failures for newly launched instances within the US-EAST-2 Region. Changes to instance networking state, such as mapping Elastic IP addresses, may also experience increased error rates. Some AWS services, such as Elastic Load Balancing and Elastic Map Reduce, experienced provisioning delays due to failures in launching new EC2 instances.

We are investigating increased EC2 launch failures and networking connectivity issues for some instances in a single Availability Zone (USE1-AZ4) in the US-EAST-1 Region. We are also experiencing elevated RunInstance API error rates for launches within the affected Availability Zone. We have a small number of affected EBS volumes that are still experiencing degraded IO performance that we are working to recover.
customers experienced Internet connectivity issues for some networks to and from the US-EAST-2 Region. Some customers may be experiencing VPN connectivity due to this issue.
customers experienced increased DNS query timeouts in a single Availability Zone (use1-az6) of the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced intermittent delays in propagating CloudFront distribution changes to some edge locations.

Between May 10 10:50 AM and May 11 2:18 AM PDT we experienced increased change propagation latency and elevated error rates for CreateHostedZone, DeleteHostedZone, AssociateVPCWithHostedZone and DisassociateVPCFromHostedZone calls to the Route 53 API.
customers may have experienced longer than usual propagation times while making changes to CloudFront configurations.
Between 7:34 PM and 8:45 PM PST we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits.
we experienced delays in propagating changes to CloudFront distributions to our edge locations

We are investigating elevated error rates and elevated latency in multiple edge locations. We can confirm elevated error rates and high latency accessing content from multiple Edge Locations, which is also contributing to longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations. During this time, customers may also have experienced longer than usual change propagation delays for CloudFront configurations and invalidations.
Between 7:49 PM and 8:45 PM PST the Route 53 Management Console was experiencing elevated error rates.
we experienced elevated DNS resolution issues for some instances in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations.

We experienced longer than usual reporting delays for Invalidations and CloudFront configurations to edge locations. Customer changes were propagating normally across our edge locations during this time but the associated reporting was not getting updated correctly. Between 5:41 AM and 11:26 PM PST, CloudFront Real-time Metrics were not available.
This is causing DNS lookups through these resolvers for a small number of AWS names to fail.
we experienced delays in propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations.

We experienced increased change propagation latency and elevated error rates for CreateHostedZone, DeleteHostedZone, AssociateVPCWithHostedZone and DisassociateVPCFromHostedZone calls to the Route 53 API.
we experienced increased delays in validation and issuance for DNS validated domains in the US-EAST-1 Region. Certificates can be requested but will be delayed in issuance.
Users experienced elevated SSL Handshake and BadRequest errors to some CloudFront edge locations.
customers experienced delays propagating changes submitted to the Route 53 API, as well as increased API error rates
we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers.

We are investigating reports that domain nameservers may unintentionally change for domains registered under .ch, .com.au, .co.nz, .es, .im, .it, .jp, .net.au, .nl, .nz, .se and .sh, which are registered through our third party registrar partner.
we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers.

We are actively investigating intermittent DNS errors resolving some CloudFront distributions in Japan. Between 8:55 AM and 10:25 AM PDT, some customers in Japan had a small percentage of DNS lookups fail when resolving CloudFront distributions.
customers may have experienced longer than usual propagation times while making changes to CloudFront configurations.
we experienced increased errors when making changes to some hosted zones, and creating new hosted zones using the Route 53 API and Console.

customers might have experienced delays in reporting status change of CloudFront invalidations. During this time, all CloudFront edge locations were consuming configuration changes and invalidations normally but were not updating status changes in console or via CloudFront APIs.

We are investigating longer than usual propagation times to propagate certain configuration changes to a few of our edge locations: the creation and deletion of CloudFront distributions and the updating of certificates for a CloudFront distribution.

We are currently investigating intermittent errors connecting to some custom non-S3 Origins from our Regional Edge Caches. We are investigating an intermittent issue resulting in errors resolving origin domains hosted at an external provider outside of the AWS network.

Customers experienced issues resolving newly created public EC2 instance names to private IPs within newly created VPCs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Creation of new Route 53 Resolver Endpoints was also delayed during this time.
we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits to a single Route 53 edge location.
We are investigating slow propagation of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers. Requests to the ChangeResourceRecordSets API are currently being throttled.
we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits.
